page: Amazon Web Services r5d.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It features 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with high ratings in multi-core CPU stress tests, Passmark CPU scores, and software compilation tasks. The memory subsystem demonstrates strong performance, achieving high read and write bandwidths in large-block tests. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, alongside high static web serving throughput. However, single-core CPU performance and single-threaded tasks remain in the average range.

While the instance excels at memory-intensive and parallelized workloads, it exhibits weak performance in LLM text generation tasks, particularly with larger models, due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The qualitative cost profile reflects a high-density resource allocation suited for enterprise-grade deployments. This server is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive data processing applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server designed for high-density workloads. It is equipped with 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance features 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network speed of 25 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU capabilities, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional performance in database and Redis operations. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation is weak due to the absence of GPU acceleration. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for large databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring high-capacity DDR4 RAM and local NVMe storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory for memory-intensive applications
3600 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed local I/O
Strong performance in database operations, Redis caching, and multi-core tasks
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for reliable data transfer
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and enterprise workloads
